description: Build a self-improving knowledge base (village elder) that ingests sources, maintains cross-linked wiki pages, answers queries, and generates VOICE.md for agent consumption. Inspired by Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern. Uses MemoryStore from Epic 1 as foundation.
title: Village Elder — Self-improving knowledge base

close_reason: Implemented ElderStore with ingestion pipeline, content extraction (URL via trafilatura, local .md/.txt files), auto-tagging, log.md tracking, and ingest-to-processed file movement. 6 passing tests.
description:
Build the Elder knowledge base store extending MemoryStore with ingestion capabilities.

**File:** village/elder.py

**ElderStore(MemoryStore):**
- __init__(wiki_path: Path) — path to wiki/ directory
- see(source: str | Path) -> IngestResult — ingest URL or file, create summary, index, cross-link
- ask(question: str) -> AskResult — query wiki, synthesize answer (future task, stub for now)

**IngestResult dataclass:**
- entry_id: str
- title: str
- related_count: int (number of pages cross-linked)
- status: str (success, error)

**Wiki directory structure:**
```
wiki/
├── ingest/        # Drop sources here (monitored)
├── processed/     # Moved after successful ingestion
├── pages/         # LLM-generated wiki pages (inherits MemoryStore entries/)
│   ├── auth-setup.md
│   └── configuration.md
├── index.md       # Auto-generated catalog
└── log.md         # Chronological record
```

**Ingestion pipeline (see method):**
1. Detect source type (URL vs file)
2. Extract content:
   - URL: fetch via httpx, convert HTML→markdown (trafilatura)
   - .md/.txt: read directly
   - .pdf: extract text (stdlib or minimal lib)
3. Generate summary via LLM (reuse village/llm/ module)
4. Create wiki page in pages/ with YAML frontmatter
5. Append to log.md with timestamp and source
6. Update index.md
7. Trigger cross-linking (delegates to crosslink module, bd-jv0.2)

**Dependency:** Add trafilatura to pyproject.toml

**Tests:** tests/test_elder.py
- Test see with local markdown file
- Test see with text file
- Test see with invalid source
- Test log.md appended correctly
- Test index.md updated correctly
- Test processed file moved to processed/
title: Build ElderStore — ingestion pipeline and content extraction

close_reason: Implemented cross-linking engine with find_related (tag Jaccard + title keyword overlap scoring), update_cross_references (bidirectional frontmatter updates), and suggest_links (missing connection detection). 35 tests passing.
description:
Find related wiki pages and maintain bidirectional cross-references. Called by ElderStore.see() after each ingestion.

**File:** village/elder/crosslink.py

**Functions:**
- find_related(store: MemoryStore, entry: MemoryEntry, k: int = 5) -> list[tuple[MemoryEntry, float]]
  — Score entries by tag overlap and text similarity. Returns (entry, score) tuples.
  — Scoring: tag_jaccard * 0.6 + title_keyword_overlap * 0.4
  — Filter out score < 0.2

- update_cross_references(store: MemoryStore, source_id: str, related_ids: list[str]) -> int
  — Add source_id to related field of each related entry
  — Add related_ids to related field of source entry
  — Returns count of pages updated

**Cross-reference format in frontmatter:**
```yaml
related: [auth-setup, configuration, worktree-lifecycle]
```

**Wiki-link format in page body:**
```markdown
## Related
- [[auth-setup]] — Environment variables for auth setup
- [[configuration]] — Project configuration overview
```

**Tests:** tests/test_crosslink.py
- Test find_related with tag overlap
- Test find_related with no related entries
- Test update_cross_references bidirectional
- Test scoring thresholds
title: Build cross-linking engine

close_reason: Implemented Curator class in village/elder/curate.py with find_orphans, find_stale, check_links, generate_voice, and curate methods. Added 19 tests in tests/test_curate.py covering all methods including mocked HTTP checks.
description:
Health checks and maintenance for the wiki. Finds stale content, orphans, contradictions, and missing cross-references.

**File:** village/elder/curate.py

**CurateResult dataclass:**
- broken_links: list[BrokenLink] — URLs that return 404
- orphans: list[str] — page IDs with no inbound references
- stale_entries: list[StaleEntry] — entries older than threshold with no recent verification
- contradictions: list[Contradiction] — conflicting claims between pages (LLM-powered)
- missing_links: list[MissingLink] — pages that should reference each other but don't
- voice_updated: bool — whether VOICE.md was regenerated

**Curator class:**
- __init__(store: ElderStore, config: CurateConfig)
- check_links() -> list[BrokenLink] — HTTP HEAD for URL sources
- find_orphans() -> list[str] — entries with no inbound related refs
- find_stale(max_age_days: int = 90) -> list[StaleEntry]
- find_contradictions() -> list[Contradiction] — LLM analyzes pairs of related pages
- suggest_links() -> list[MissingLink] — pages with tag overlap but no cross-ref
- curate() -> CurateResult — run all checks + regenerate VOICE.md

**VOICE.md generation:**
After curate runs, regenerate VOICE.md at project root:
```markdown
# Village Voice — <project name>
> Last updated: 2026-04-08 by Village Elder

## Summary
<LLM-distilled 1-2 paragraph overview>

## Recent Activity
- [date] Brief description (see: page-id)

## Conventions
- Bulleted list of project conventions from wiki

## Known Gotchas
- Bulleted list of gotchas/mistakes from wiki

## Active Questions
- Unresolved questions from wiki

## Wiki Index
- [page-id] — One-line summary
```

**CurateConfig:**
- stale_threshold_days: int = 90
- check_urls: bool = True
- llm_analyze: bool = True (for contradictions)

**Tests:** tests/test_curate.py
- Test find_orphans with known orphan
- Test find_orphans with fully connected wiki
- Test suggest_links finds missing connections
- Test VOICE.md generation
- Test check_links with mock HTTP
title: Build curation engine (curate/upkeep)

close_reason: Implemented ask method on ElderStore with AskResult dataclass. Queries wiki via keyword search, returns concatenated relevant page excerpts. Supports save=True to persist synthesized answers as new wiki pages. Added 5 tests covering matching, no-match, empty wiki, save, and source population.
description:
Implement the ask method on ElderStore. Queries the wiki using keyword search, reads relevant pages, and synthesizes an answer via LLM.

**File:** village/elder.py (add to existing ElderStore class)

**AskResult dataclass:**
- answer: str — synthesized answer text
- sources: list[str] — page IDs used as source material
- saved: bool — whether the answer was saved as a new page

**ElderStore.ask(question: str, save: bool = False) -> AskResult:**
1. store.find(question, k=5) — keyword search for relevant pages
2. Read full text of top-k matching pages
3. Call LLM with: question + page contents as context
4. LLM synthesizes answer with citations ([page-id])
5. If save=True, create new wiki page from the answer
6. Return AskResult

**LLM integration:**
- Reuse village/llm/ module (same client as chat)
- System prompt: 'You are a knowledge base assistant. Answer based on the provided wiki pages. Cite sources as [page-id]. If unsure, say so.'
- Configurable model via [memory] config or fallback to default

**Tests:** tests/test_elder.py (add to existing)
- Test ask with matching pages
- Test ask with no matching pages
- Test ask with save=True creates new page
- Test citations in answer
title: Build ask command — query wiki and synthesize answers

close_reason: Built CLI commands: see, fetch, ask, curate, upkeep, stats, monitor
description:
CLI subcommand group for village elder. Wires ElderStore to Click commands.

**File:** village/cli/elder.py

**Commands:**

```bash
village elder see <url|file> [--tags tag1,tag2]   # Ingest + cross-link
village elder fetch <url|file> [--tags tag1,tag2]  # Alias for see
village elder ask <question> [--save]              # Query + synthesize
village elder curate                               # Health check + repair + regenerate VOICE.md
village elder upkeep                               # Alias for curate
village elder stats                                # Show wiki stats (page count, recent activity, orphans)
village elder monitor [--interval 30]              # Poll wiki/ingest/ for new files
```

**Register in village/cli/__init__.py:**
```python
from village.cli import elder
village.add_command(elder.elder_group)
```

**Implementation details:**
- see/fetch: Parse source, call ElderStore.see(), print result
- ask: Call ElderStore.ask(), print answer and sources
- curate/upkeep: Call Curator.curate(), print findings, note VOICE.md update
- stats: Print page count, recent entries, orphan count, last curated date
- monitor: Poll wiki/ingest/ every N seconds, process new files via ElderStore.see()

**Output format:** Use click.echo(), support --json flag where appropriate.

**Tests:** tests/test_cli_elder.py
- Test see command with local file
- Test ask command output
- Test stats command output
- Test curate command output
title: Build CLI commands for village elder

close_reason: Built polling-based Monitor class with state persistence
description:
Simple polling-based file watcher for wiki/ingest/ directory. No additional dependencies (no watchdog).

**File:** village/elder/monitor.py

**Monitor class:**
- __init__(wiki_path: Path, store: ElderStore, poll_interval: int = 30)
- start() -> None — blocking loop, poll ingest/ directory
- stop() -> None — signal to stop polling
- poll() -> list[IngestResult] — single pass: list new files, ingest each

**Implementation:**
- Track seen files in .village/monitor_state.json (just a set of filenames)
- Each poll: os.listdir(wiki/ingest/) - seen_files = new_files
- For each new file: call store.see(file)
- Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
- Configurable poll_interval via [memory] config or CLI --interval flag

**No dependencies** — uses os.listdir, time.sleep, signal, json

**Tests:** tests/test_monitor.py
- Test poll with new file
- Test poll with no new files
- Test poll with multiple new files
- Test seen state persistence
- Test graceful stop
title: Build monitor — polling-based file watcher for wiki/ingest/

description:
End-to-end integration tests covering complete Elder workflows.

**File:** tests/test_elder_integration.py

**Test scenarios:**
1. **Full ingest flow:** drop file in wiki/ingest/ → see processes it → page created in wiki/pages/ → index.md updated → log.md updated → cross-links added → file moved to processed/
2. **Query flow:** ingest multiple sources → ask question → answer cites sources → save creates new page
3. **Curate flow:** create wiki with orphans and stale entries → curate finds issues → VOICE.md regenerated
4. **Monitor flow:** drop files → monitor picks them up → ingested → state persisted
5. **Config integration:** MemoryConfig loads from config file → ElderStore uses configured paths

**Test fixtures:** tests/fixtures/elder/
- sample.md — simple markdown source
- sample.txt — plain text source
- sample-with-links.md — markdown with cross-references

**Use tmp_path for all file operations.**
title: Integration tests for Elder end-to-end flows

close_reason: Built MemoryStore in village/memory.py with put/get/find/recent/related/delete/rebuild_index. 23 tests passing.
description:
Core memory abstraction that stores entries as individual markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Serves both agent memory (.village/memory/) and Elder KB (wiki/).

**File:** village/memory.py

**MemoryStore class:**
- __init__(store_path: Path) — e.g. .village/memory/ or wiki/
- put(title, text, tags=[], metadata={}, source=None) -> str — write new entry, return ID
- get(entry_id: str) -> MemoryEntry | None — retrieve single entry
- find(query: str, k: int = 5) -> list[MemoryEntry] — keyword search (case-insensitive substring over title+text+tags)
- recent(limit: int = 10) -> list[MemoryEntry] — most recent by created timestamp
- related(entry_id: str, k: int = 5) -> list[MemoryEntry] — entries with tag overlap
- all_entries() -> list[MemoryEntry] — list all entries
- delete(entry_id: str) -> bool — remove entry
- rebuild_index() -> None — regenerate index.md from entries

**MemoryEntry dataclass:**
- id: str (auto-generated, e.g. note-001)
- title: str
- text: str
- tags: list[str]
- created: datetime
- metadata: dict (session, source, related entries, etc.)

**Entry file format (markdown + YAML frontmatter):**
```markdown
---
id: note-001
tags: [auth, configuration, gotcha]
created: 2026-04-08T10:30:00
session: abc123
source: https://docs.example.com/auth
related: [note-003, note-007]
---

# Title here

Entry content goes here.
```

**index.md format:**
```markdown
# Memory Index

- [note-001] Title here — auth, configuration, gotcha
- [note-002] Another entry — tags, here
```

**Storage layout:**
```
.village/memory/
├── index.md
└── entries/
    ├── note-001.md
    └── note-002.md
```

**No external dependencies** — uses stdlib only (pathlib, yaml via custom parser or stdlib, datetime, re).

**Tests:** tests/test_memory.py
- Test put/get roundtrip
- Test find by keyword
- Test recent ordering
- Test related by tag overlap
- Test rebuild_index
- Test delete
- Test empty store behavior
title: Build MemoryStore — core markdown-based read/write/search

close_reason: Implemented FileMemoryContext, MemoryConfig, and factory function with full test coverage (7 tests).
description:
Replace MemvidChatContext with FileMemoryContext backed by MemoryStore. Wire into existing chat extension system.

**File:** village/extensibility/file_memory_context.py

**FileMemoryContext(ChatContext):**
- __init__(memory_path: Path) — path to .village/memory/
- load_context(session_id) -> SessionContext — load recent memories + find relevant by session metadata
- save_context(context) -> None — write pending_memories to MemoryStore
- enrich_context(context) -> SessionContext — pass-through for now

**Factory function:**
- create_file_memory_context(config) -> ChatContext — returns FileMemoryContext if memory enabled, else DefaultChatContext

**Config changes (village/config.py):**
- Add MemoryConfig dataclass: enabled (bool), store_path (str, default .village/memory/)
- Replace MemvidConfig with MemoryConfig in Config dataclass
- Parse [memory] section from config file
- Support env vars: VILLAGE_MEMORY_ENABLED, VILLAGE_MEMORY_PATH

**Wiring:**
- The factory function integrates with existing extension loader
- Extension can be activated via VILLAGE_EXTENSIONS_CHAT_CONTEXT_MODULE config
- Or auto-activated when config.memory.enabled is true

**Tests:** tests/test_file_memory_context.py
- Test load_context with empty store
- Test load_context with existing memories
- Test save_context writes entries
- Test factory returns DefaultChatContext when disabled
- Test factory returns FileMemoryContext when enabled
title: Build FileMemoryContext and wire into chat lifecycle
